---
title: "Protocol Template"
author: "Janis Sonnemann"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: yes
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
header-includes:
- \usepackage{pdfpages}
- \usepackage{float}
- \usepackage{fancyhdr}
- \usepackage{lastpage}
- \pagestyle{fancy}
# Put project name here 
- \fancyhead[C]{MPO-mRNA}
# put experiment name here
- \fancyhead[R]{Exp1}
- \fancyfoot[C]{\thepage\ von \pageref{LastPage}}
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
#   replace XXX with experiment name
knit: (function(input, ...) {JanisHelpers::knit_multiple_dated(input, path = "protocols/XXX", format = "pdf_document")})
---

```{r setup, include = knitr::is_html_output(), error = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.path = paste0("output/protocols/", protocol_name, "figs/", Sys.Date(),"/"), dev = "png", 
                      warning = FALSE,
                      # hides code in pdf file
                      echo = knitr::is_html_output())

options(knitr.kable.NA = '')

librarian::shelf(tidyverse, readxl, here, kableExtra, httr, jsonlite)



```


```{r get-protocol, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}

source(file = here("src", "get_protocol.R"))

# Set name of protocol
protocol_name <- "name"
# Paste ID of protocol, comes after: https://www.protocols.io/edit/ e.g. https://www.protocols.io/edit/mpo-tetramer-testing5-cnq7vdzn 
protocol_ID <- "ID"


# Function to get and convert protocol
get_pdf_protocol(protocol_name = protocol_name,
             protocol_ID = protocol_ID)

pdf_file <- paste0("output/protocols/", protocol_name, "/raw/", Sys.Date(), "-", protocol_name, ".pdf")

include_protocol <- paste0("\\includepdf[pages=-, scale = 1.1, pagecommand={}] {", pdf_file, "}")

```

`r include_protocol`
